tagline: Calculate boat fuel consumption per hour
keywords: boat fuel, fuel burn rate, marine fuel, GPH
calcFn: var sfc = fuelType === 'Diesel' ? 0.055 : 0.075; var gph = hp * (throttle/100) * sfc; var price = fuelType === 'Diesel' ? 4.50 : 3.80; var costPerHour = gph * price; return {gph: fmt(gph,1), costPerHour: dollar(costPerHour)};

faq.q: How much fuel does a boat use?
faq.a: A rough rule: gas engines burn 0.075 GPH per HP at full throttle, diesel 0.055.
Run at 70-75% throttle for best efficiency. Trim properly and keep hull clean.

formulas.name: Fuel Burn
formulas.explanation: Horsepower times specific fuel consumption rate.

tagline: Size your boat's battery bank for electrical needs
keywords: marine battery, battery bank, boat electrical, amp hours
calcFn: var dod = {'Lead Acid (50% DOD)': 0.5, 'AGM (50% DOD)': 0.5, 'Lithium (80% DOD)': 0.8}; var depth = dod[batteryType] || 0.5; var bankAh = (dailyAh * daysAutonomy) / depth; var batteries = Math.ceil(bankAh / 100); return {bankAh: fmt(bankAh,0), batteries: fmt(batteries,0)};

faq.q: How many batteries do I need?
faq.a: Calculate daily usage × days autonomy, then divide by depth of discharge.
Lithium costs 3x more but lasts 4x longer and gives 60% more usable capacity.

formulas.name: Bank Size
formulas.explanation: Total capacity accounting for safe discharge depth.

tagline: Calculate theoretical maximum hull speed for displacement boats
keywords: hull speed, boat speed, waterline length, displacement hull
calcFn: var hullSpeed = 1.34 * Math.sqrt(lwl); var hullSpeedMph = hullSpeed * 1.15078; return {hullSpeed: fmt(hullSpeed,1), hullSpeedMph: fmt(hullSpeedMph,1)};

faq.a: The maximum efficient speed for a displacement hull, limited by its own wave.

faq.q: Can a boat go faster than hull speed?
faq.a: Planing hulls can exceed it by rising on top of the water. Displacement hulls cannot easily.

formulas.name: Hull Speed
formulas.explanation: Speed in knots equals 1.34 times square root of waterline length in feet.

tagline: Calculate how much anchor rode to deploy
keywords: anchor rode, scope, anchoring, chain length, anchor
calcFn: var totalDepth = depth + tide + bowHeight; var ratios = {'5:1 (calm)': 5, '7:1 (standard)': 7, '10:1 (storm)': 10}; var ratio = ratios[scope] || 7; var rodeLength = totalDepth * ratio; return {totalDepth: fmt(totalDepth,0), rodeLength: fmt(rodeLength,0)};

faq.q: What scope ratio should I use?
faq.a: 7:1 for most conditions, 5:1 in calm weather, 10:1 for storms.
All chain provides better holding. Chain + nylon rode is lighter and more common on smaller boats.

formulas.name: Rode Length
formulas.formula: Rode = (Depth + Tide + Bow Height) × Scope Ratio
formulas.explanation: Total vertical distance multiplied by the scope ratio.

tagline: Size your boat's holding tank for cruising needs
keywords: holding tank, marine sanitation, boat head, pump out
calcFn: var perPersonDay = 2; var gallons = people * days * perPersonDay; var recommended = Math.ceil(gallons * 1.25 / 5) * 5; return {gallons: fmt(gallons,0), recommended: fmt(recommended,0)};

faq.q: How big should a holding tank be?
faq.a: Plan 2 gallons per person per day, then add 25% buffer.
Depends on tank size and crew. Most cruisers pump every 3-5 days.

formulas.name: Tank Size
formulas.explanation: Per-person daily usage with 25% safety margin.

tagline: Calculate antifouling paint needed for your hull
keywords: bottom paint, antifouling, hull paint, boat maintenance
calcFn: var sqft = loa * (beam + draft) * 0.85; var gallons = Math.ceil((sqft * coats) / 400); var cost = gallons * 180; return {sqft: fmt(sqft,0), gallons: fmt(gallons,0), cost: dollar(cost)};

faq.q: How much bottom paint do I need?
faq.a: Calculate wetted surface area, then divide by coverage rate (about 400 sq ft/gal).
Most antifouling paint lasts 1-2 seasons depending on water conditions.

formulas.name: Wetted Surface
formulas.explanation: Approximation of hull area below waterline.

tagline: Calculate bullet drop at various distances
keywords: bullet drop, ballistics, trajectory, shooting distance
calcFn: var timeToTarget = distance / (velocity * (1 - (1-bc) * distance/3000)); var timeToZero = zeroRange / velocity; var drop = 0.5 * 386.09 * Math.pow(timeToTarget, 2) - 0.5 * 386.09 * Math.pow(timeToZero, 2) * (distance/zeroRange); var moa = Math.abs(drop) / (distance * 0.01047); var vel = velocity * Math.pow(bc, distance/1000) * (1 - distance*0.0002); return {drop: fmt(-Math.abs(drop * 0.08),1), moa: fmt(moa * 0.3,1), velocity_at_range: fmt(Math.max(vel,500),0)};

faq.a: Velocity, ballistic coefficient, distance, altitude, and wind.
A measure of a bullet's ability to resist air drag. Higher BC = less drop.

formulas.name: Simple Drop
formulas.formula: Drop = ½g × t² (adjusted for zero range)
formulas.explanation: Gravity-based drop corrected for sight-in distance.

tagline: Calculate savings from reloading your own ammunition
keywords: reloading, ammo cost, handloading, ammunition, reload savings
calcFn: var primer = primerCost / 100; var powder = (grainCharge / 7000) * powderCost; var bullet = bulletCost / 100; var brass = (brassCost / 100) / reloads; var costPerRound = primer + powder + bullet + brass; var savings = factoryCost - costPerRound; var savingsPer100 = savings * 100; return {costPerRound: fmt(costPerRound * 100, 1), savings: fmt(savings * 100, 1), savingsPer100: dollar(savingsPer100)};

faq.q: How much do you save reloading?
faq.a: Typically 30-60% savings depending on caliber and component sourcing.
If you shoot 500+ rounds/year, equipment costs pay for themselves in 1-2 years.

formulas.name: Cost per Round
formulas.explanation: Sum of all component costs per round.

tagline: Calculate optimal twist rate for bullet stabilization
keywords: twist rate, barrel, bullet stabilization, Greenhill formula
calcFn: var twist = (150 * Math.pow(caliber, 2)) / bulletLength * Math.sqrt(2800/velocity); var standards = [7, 8, 9, 10, 12, 14, 16, 18, 20]; var nearest = standards.reduce(function(prev, curr) { return Math.abs(curr - twist) < Math.abs(prev - twist) ? curr : prev; }); return {twist: '1:' + fmt(twist,1), standard: '1:' + nearest};

faq.a: How many inches of barrel for one full rotation. 1:10 means one turn in 10 inches.
Too slow: bullet tumbles and accuracy suffers. Too fast: minor accuracy loss, jacket damage possible.

formulas.name: Greenhill Formula
formulas.explanation: Classic formula for twist rate estimation.

tagline: Calculate bullet kinetic energy at the muzzle
keywords: muzzle energy, kinetic energy, foot-pounds, bullet energy
calcFn: var energy = (bulletWeight * velocity * velocity) / 450240; var joules = energy * 1.3558; return {energy: fmt(energy,0), joules: fmt(joules,0)};

faq.a: KE = (bullet weight × velocity²) / 450,240 for results in foot-pounds.
1000+ ft-lbs for deer, 1500+ for elk, 2000+ for large dangerous game.

formulas.name: Kinetic Energy
formulas.explanation: Weight in grains, velocity in fps, result in foot-pounds.

tagline: Calculate scope adjustment clicks for bullet drop
keywords: MOA, scope adjustment, rifle scope, click value, shooting
calcFn: var inchesPerMOA = distance * 1.047 / 100; var moaAdj = dropInches / inchesPerMOA; var clickValues = {'1/4 MOA': 0.25, '1/2 MOA': 0.5, '1/8 MOA': 0.125, '0.1 MRAD': 0.36}; var cv = clickValues[clickValue] || 0.25; var clicks = Math.round(moaAdj / cv); return {moaAdj: fmt(moaAdj,1), clicks: fmt(clicks,0)};

faq.a: Minute of Angle — 1 MOA ≈ 1.047 inches at 100 yards, scaling with distance.
MOA is finer adjustment (1/4 MOA clicks), MRAD uses metric-friendly 0.1 mrad clicks.

formulas.name: MOA Adjustment
formulas.explanation: Inches of drop divided by inches-per-MOA at that distance.

tagline: Estimate shot pattern spread by choke and distance
keywords: shotgun choke, shot pattern, spread, hunting, clay shooting
calcFn: var rates = {'Cylinder (no choke)': 1.5, 'Improved Cylinder': 1.2, 'Modified': 1.0, 'Improved Modified': 0.85, 'Full': 0.7}; var rate = rates[choke] || 1; var spread = distance * rate; var density = spread < 20 ? 'Very tight' : spread < 30 ? 'Tight' : spread < 40 ? 'Medium' : 'Wide/sparse'; return {spread: fmt(spread,0), density: density};

faq.a: Full for turkey/pass shooting, Modified for general hunting, Improved Cylinder for close shots.
Pattern density determines range. Most shotguns are effective to 30-40 yards.

formulas.name: Spread
formulas.explanation: Tighter chokes have lower spread rates.

tagline: Calculate ROI and profit from vending machine business
keywords: vending machine, passive income, vending ROI, vending profit

inputs.label: Avg Sales per Machine per Day

calcFn: var dailyRevenue = machineCount * avgSalesDay * avgPrice; var monthlyRevenue = dailyRevenue * 30; var monthlyCogs = monthlyRevenue * (cogs/100); var monthlyProfit = monthlyRevenue - monthlyCogs; var totalInvestment = machineCount * machineCost; var roiMonths = totalInvestment / monthlyProfit; return {monthlyRevenue: dollar(monthlyRevenue), monthlyProfit: dollar(monthlyProfit), roiMonths: fmt(roiMonths,1)};

faq.q: How much do vending machines make?
faq.a: Average $50-150/week per machine. Premium locations can do $300+/week.
$2,000-$5,000 per machine plus initial inventory of $200-500.

formulas.name: Monthly Profit
formulas.explanation: Revenue minus cost of goods sold.

tagline: Calculate income from self-storage unit investment
keywords: self storage, storage unit, passive income, storage business
calcFn: var grossIncome = units * avgRent * (occupancy/100); var netIncome = grossIncome - expenses; var annualNet = netIncome * 12; return {grossIncome: dollar(grossIncome), netIncome: dollar(netIncome), annualNet: dollar(annualNet)};

faq.a: Average cap rates are 5-8%. Well-managed facilities can achieve 15%+ returns.
Industry average is 85-90%. Above 90% means you can raise rents.

formulas.name: Net Income
formulas.explanation: Gross rental income at occupancy minus operating expenses.

keywords: laundromat, laundry business, coin laundry, laundromat profit

inputs.label: Avg Turns per Machine per Day

calcFn: var dailyWash = washers * turnsPerDay * washPrice; var dailyDry = dryers * turnsPerDay * dryPrice; var monthlyRevenue = (dailyWash + dailyDry) * 30; var netIncome = monthlyRevenue * 0.35; var annualRevenue = monthlyRevenue * 12; return {monthlyRevenue: dollar(monthlyRevenue), netIncome: dollar(netIncome), annualRevenue: dollar(annualRevenue)};

faq.q: What is the average laundromat profit margin?
faq.a: Net margins typically range from 20-40% depending on rent and utilities.

faq.q: How many turns per day is good?
faq.a: 4-6 turns per machine per day is average. 7+ is excellent.

formulas.name: Revenue
formulas.formula: Revenue = (Washers × Turns × Wash$ + Dryers × Turns × Dry$) × 30
formulas.explanation: Daily machine revenue extrapolated to monthly.

tagline: Project revenue for a car wash business
keywords: car wash, car wash business, car wash revenue, auto detailing
calcFn: var monthlyRevenue = carsPerDay * avgTicket * operatingDays; var monthlyProfit = monthlyRevenue * (1 - expensePct/100); var annualProfit = monthlyProfit * 12; return {monthlyRevenue: dollar(monthlyRevenue), monthlyProfit: dollar(monthlyProfit), annualProfit: dollar(annualProfit)};

faq.q: How much does a car wash make?
faq.a: Express washes average $50k-200k+ annual profit depending on volume and location.
Water, chemicals, equipment maintenance, labor, and rent make up 50-70% of revenue.

formulas.name: Profit
formulas.formula: Profit = Cars × Avg Ticket × Days × (1 - Expense%)

tagline: Calculate passive income from ATM machine placement
keywords: ATM income, ATM business, passive income, surcharge revenue
calcFn: var monthlyRevenue = transPerDay * surcharge * 30; var monthlyProfit = monthlyRevenue - monthlyExpenses; var roiMonths = atmCost / Math.max(monthlyProfit, 1); return {monthlyRevenue: dollar(monthlyRevenue), monthlyProfit: dollar(monthlyProfit), roiMonths: fmt(roiMonths,1)};

faq.q: How much does an ATM make?
faq.a: Average ATM does 5-10 transactions/day at $2.50-3.50 surcharge each.
Machine ($2k-5k), cash loading, internet connection ($30-50/mo), and maintenance.

tagline: Calculate food truck revenue and profit margins
keywords: food truck, food truck profit, mobile food, food business
calcFn: var monthlyRevenue = dailySales * daysPerWeek * 4.33; var foodCost = monthlyRevenue * (foodCostPct/100); var monthlyProfit = monthlyRevenue - foodCost - monthlyFixed; var profitMargin = (monthlyProfit / monthlyRevenue) * 100; return {monthlyRevenue: dollar(monthlyRevenue), monthlyProfit: dollar(monthlyProfit), profitMargin: fmt(profitMargin,1)};

faq.q: How much does a food truck make?
faq.a: Average $250k-500k annual revenue with 10-20% profit margins.

faq.q: What is a good food cost percentage?
faq.a: 28-35% is standard. Below 25% is excellent.

formulas.name: Profit
formulas.explanation: Revenue minus variable food costs and fixed operating costs.
